tasks.subject: [additional-agent-runtimes] [P1] Add Session.runtime field and 'opencode' to the runtime enum
tasks.description:
Add a required `runtime: string` field to the shared `Session` type and add `'opencode'` to the discovery enum, then make the two existing adapters populate the new field so typecheck stays green.

FILES:
- `packages/shared/src/schemas.ts` — `SessionSchema` is at lines 106-120. Add `runtime: z.string()` to the object (place it after `permissionMode`). Resulting shape:
```ts
export const SessionSchema = z
  .object({
    id: z.string().uuid(),
    title: z.string(),
    createdAt: z.string().datetime(),
    updatedAt: z.string().datetime(),
    lastMessagePreview: z.string().optional(),
    permissionMode: PermissionModeSchema,
    runtime: z.string(),
    model: z.string().optional(),
    effort: EffortLevelSchema.optional(),
    fastMode: z.boolean().optional(),
    contextTokens: z.number().int().optional(),
    cwd: z.string().optional(),
  })
  .openapi('Session');
```
- `packages/shared/src/mesh-schemas.ts` — `AgentRuntimeSchema` enum is at lines 18-36. Insert `'opencode'` into the `z.enum([...])` array (it currently has `'codex'` but NOT `'opencode'`). Add a TSDoc note above the enum documenting its DUAL use: discovery `detectedRuntime` (which harness created an agent dir) vs execution runtime (which backend DorkOS runs the agent with). Do NOT fork the enum.
- `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/claude-code/` — wherever `listSessions`/`getSession` build `Session` objects, set `runtime: 'claude-code'` on each returned object (spec §Architecture: "claude-code fills it today from its own type"). Grep for the transcript reader that constructs Session objects.
- `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/test-mode/session-registry.ts` — its `list()`/`get()` build `Session` objects; set `runtime: 'test-mode'`.

ACCEPTANCE:
- `pnpm --filter @dorkos/shared build` and `pnpm typecheck` pass (the required field forces every Session producer to set it — this is intentional).
- Any test fixture that constructs a bare `Session` gains `runtime`.

TESTS: extend existing schema round-trip tests in `packages/shared` if present; assert `SessionSchema.parse(session).runtime` is required (parse of a session without `runtime` throws).
tasks.activeForm: Adding Session.runtime field and opencode enum value

tasks.subject: [additional-agent-runtimes] [P1] Add runtimes.* user-config block with a semver conf migration
tasks.description:
Add a `runtimes` section to `UserConfigSchema` and append an idempotent `conf` migration to backfill it for existing configs. Follow the `adding-config-fields` skill and `contributing/configuration.md` (Schema Migrations).

FILES:
- `packages/shared/src/config-schema.ts` — add the `runtimes` block to `UserConfigSchema` (the object ends around line 213 with `sessionSecret`). Target JSON shape (from spec §Data model changes):
```jsonc
"runtimes": {
  "default": "claude-code",   // registry default at boot
  "opencode": {
    "enabled": true,
    "binaryPath": null,        // null -> resolve from PATH
    "port": 0                  // 0 -> ephemeral port
  },
  "codex": {
    "enabled": true,
    "binaryPath": null
  }
}
```
Implement as Zod, matching the surrounding `.default(() => ({...}))` convention used by `server`, `workspace`, `harness`:
```ts
runtimes: z
  .object({
    default: z.string().default('claude-code'),
    opencode: z
      .object({
        enabled: z.boolean().default(true),
        binaryPath: z.string().nullable().default(null),
        port: z.number().int().min(0).max(65535).default(0),
      })
      .default(() => ({ enabled: true, binaryPath: null, port: 0 })),
    codex: z
      .object({
        enabled: z.boolean().default(true),
        binaryPath: z.string().nullable().default(null),
      })
      .default(() => ({ enabled: true, binaryPath: null })),
  })
  .default(() => ({
    default: 'claude-code',
    opencode: { enabled: true, binaryPath: null, port: 0 },
    codex: { enabled: true, binaryPath: null },
  })),
```
- `apps/server/src/services/core/config-manager.ts` — append one migration to the `CONFIG_MIGRATIONS` map (the chain currently ends at `'0.46.0': backfillHarnessDefaults`). Write a `backfillRuntimesDefaults(store)` function mirroring `backfillHarnessDefaults` (lines ~130-147): guard with `if (store.get('runtimes') == null)` then `store.set('runtimes', { default: 'claude-code', opencode: { enabled: true, binaryPath: null, port: 0 }, codex: { enabled: true, binaryPath: null } })`. Mark it `@internal Exported for testing only.` Key it at the next ascending release placeholder `'0.47.0'` (reconciled to the concrete version at tag time by `/system:release` — the same pattern the `<next-release>` comments describe). Never edit a shipped migration body.

ACCEPTANCE:
- `USER_CONFIG_DEFAULTS` (parsed from `{ version: 1 }`) now contains `runtimes.default === 'claude-code'`.
- A config file written before this change, loaded through ConfigManager, gains the `runtimes` object.
- `z.toJSONSchema(UserConfigSchema)` still succeeds (conf's Ajv bridge).

TESTS (config-manager `__tests__`): call `backfillRuntimesDefaults` on a store missing `runtimes` -> writes the object; call again -> idempotent no-op (guard on present key); assert `UserConfigSchema.parse({ version: 1 }).runtimes` has the expected defaults. Follow `.claude/rules/testing.md` service-test patterns.
tasks.activeForm: Adding runtimes.* config block and migration

tasks.subject: [additional-agent-runtimes] [P1] Aggregate GET /api/sessions across all registered runtimes with graceful degradation
tasks.description:
Move session LISTING from single-default-runtime to registry aggregation (ADR-0308), with graceful per-runtime degradation, and tag each session with its runtime.

FILES:
- `apps/server/src/routes/sessions.ts` — the `GET /` handler (lines 47-74) currently does `const runtime = runtimeRegistry.getDefault(); const sessions = await runtime.listSessions(projectDir);`. Replace with a fan-out across `runtimeRegistry.listRuntimes()` (method exists at `runtime-registry.ts:299`):
  - Call each runtime's `listSessions(projectDir)` inside `Promise.allSettled`, each wrapped in a per-runtime timeout (2s — see spec §Performance) so one slow/cold backend cannot stall the list.
  - Merge all fulfilled results, ensure each session carries `runtime` (adapters set it per task 1.1; defensively fill from the runtime's `type` if absent), sort by `updatedAt` descending, then `slice(0, limit)`.
  - For rejected/timed-out runtimes, collect a `warnings[]` entry (e.g. `{ runtime: type, message }`) instead of failing the request.
  - Change the response from a bare array to an envelope. Update `ListSessionsQuerySchema` consumers accordingly. Add an optional `?runtime=<type>` filter (validate against registered types; unknown -> ignore or 400 consistent with existing query validation).
  - Keep the ADR-0260 persisted-settings overlay (`getSessionSettingsMany` batch) applied to the MERGED list.
- `apps/server/src/routes/sessions.ts` `GET /:id` (lines 89-111) — include `runtime` on the returned session (resolve via `runtimeRegistry.getSessionRuntimeType(sessionId)` / the resolved runtime's `type`).
- `packages/shared/src/schemas.ts` — if the list envelope changes shape, define/extend the response schema (`warnings[]` optional). Keep the client transport (`packages/shared/src/transport.ts`) and `useSessions` hook in sync — decide whether to keep the array response and add warnings via a header, OR move to `{ sessions, warnings }`. Prefer the smallest change that carries `warnings[]`; document the choice in a code comment.

NOTE: `subscribeSessionList` fan-in is a SEPARATE task (1.4). This task is the pull/list path only.

ACCEPTANCE:
- With only claude-code (or only test-mode) registered, behavior is a no-op refactor (spec: "aggregation of one runtime is a no-op refactor").
- With two runtimes registered, the list merges both, sorted by `updatedAt`, each row tagged `runtime`.
- A runtime whose `listSessions` rejects/times out yields a `warnings[]` entry and partial results — never a 500 or blank list.

TESTS (integration, `apps/server/src/routes/__tests__/`): register two `FakeAgentRuntime` instances (from `@dorkos/test-utils`) via `runtimeRegistry`; assert merged + sorted + tagged output; make one runtime's `listSessions` reject and assert partial list + warning; assert `?runtime=` filters to one runtime. Use supertest against the app.

tasks.subject: [additional-agent-runtimes] [P1] Fan in every runtime's subscribeSessionList into the global session-list stream
tasks.description:
Move the global live session-list subscription from a single runtime to a fan-in across all registered runtimes (ADR-0308), so a second production runtime's sessions appear/update live on `GET /api/events`.

FILES:
- `apps/server/src/services/session/session-list-broadcaster.ts` — `start(runtime: AgentRuntime)` (lines ~62-99) currently calls `runtime.subscribeSessionList(ctx)` for ONE runtime and consumes one iterator. Refactor to accept the registry (or a list of runtimes) and subscribe to EACH registered runtime's `subscribeSessionList(ctx)`, merging their `SessionListEvent`s into the single broadcast fan-out. Preserve the existing invariants: the synchronous-throw guard around iterator construction (a chokidar failure in one runtime's watcher must NOT crash boot or kill the others — catch per-runtime, log, continue), and the `onProjectorStatusChange` status fan-out installed independently of the watchers.
- `apps/server/src/index.ts` — the composition root that calls `broadcaster.start(...)`. Update the call site to pass all registered runtimes (e.g. iterate `runtimeRegistry.listRuntimes()`).

ACCEPTANCE:
- With one runtime, live list behavior is unchanged (no-op refactor).
- With two runtimes registered, `session_upserted`/`session_removed`/`session_status` events from BOTH stream to `/api/events` clients.
- One runtime's watcher throwing at construction leaves the other runtime's discovery live and the server up.

TESTS (`apps/server/src/services/session/__tests__/`): drive two fake `subscribeSessionList` async iterables; assert events from both reach the broadcast callback; make one iterator throw synchronously on construction and assert the other still broadcasts and `running` state is coherent. Follow existing session-list-broadcaster test patterns.
tasks.activeForm: Fanning in session-list subscriptions across runtimes

tasks.subject: [additional-agent-runtimes] [P1] Build the runtimeConformance shared Vitest suite and run it against test-mode + claude-code
tasks.description:
Create the DX centerpiece: a single shared Vitest suite asserting the `AgentRuntime` contract, parameterized by a factory, run against `TestModeRuntime` and (mocked) `ClaudeCodeRuntime`. This is the gate every future adapter clears before its UI activates.

FILES:
- `packages/test-utils/src/runtime-conformance.ts` (NEW) — export `runtimeConformance(makeRuntime: () => AgentRuntime, opts?: {...})`. It registers a `describe` block asserting the contract from `packages/shared/src/agent-runtime.ts`:
  - Session lifecycle: `ensureSession` then `hasSession` true; `getSession`/`getInternalSessionId` shapes.
  - `sendMessage(sessionId, content)` yields well-formed `StreamEvent`s and terminates in a TERMINAL event (assert the generator completes and the last event is a turn-ending type).
  - Interrupt semantics: `interruptQuery` resolves to a boolean.
  - History round-trip: after a turn, `getMessageHistory` returns messages (or `[]` for stateless adapters — assert it does not throw and returns an array).
  - Capability-shape validity: `getCapabilities()` returns a `RuntimeCapabilities` with a string `type`, all documented booleans present, and `permissionModes` either `{ supported: false, values: [] }` or `{ supported: true, values: PermissionModeDescriptor[] }` with each descriptor having `id`+`label`.
  - Dependency-check shape: `checkDependencies()` returns `DependencyCheck[]` each with `name`, `description`, and a `status` of `'satisfied'|'missing'|'outdated'`.
- `packages/test-utils/src/index.ts` — export `runtimeConformance`.
- `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/test-mode/__tests__/conformance.test.ts` (NEW) — `runtimeConformance(() => new TestModeRuntime())`.
- `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/claude-code/__tests__/conformance.test.ts` (NEW) — run against `ClaudeCodeRuntime` with the SDK mocked (use the SDK-level scenario builders in `services/runtimes/claude-code/__tests__/sdk-scenarios.ts` — `wrapSdkQuery`, `sdkSimpleText`; these live inside the SDK ESLint boundary). NEVER require the real `claude` binary.

DESIGN NOTE: the suite must not bake in JSONL/file assumptions — `TestModeRuntime` (stateless, EventLog-backed) and claude-code (JSONL) must BOTH pass the same assertions. Allow `opts` to declare capability expectations (e.g. `expectHistory: boolean`) so a stateless runtime that legitimately returns `[]` history is not failed.

ACCEPTANCE:
- `pnpm vitest run` green for both conformance test files.
- Adding a new method to `AgentRuntime` that a runtime omits fails compilation of that runtime (existing `FakeAgentRuntime` invariant) — conformance covers runtime BEHAVIOR, the interface covers SHAPE.

TESTS: this task IS tests; verify both suites pass and that intentionally breaking a `getCapabilities()` return (e.g. dropping `permissionModes`) makes the suite red.

tasks.subject: [additional-agent-runtimes] [P1] Add the RuntimeDescriptor client registry, OpenCode/Codex icons, and session-list row marks
tasks.description:
Create ONE source of truth for every runtime's visual identity (icon + label + accent) and use it to render a runtime mark on session-list rows. This registry backs every badge/picker in later tasks.

FILES:
- `packages/icons/src/adapter-logos.tsx` — add `OpenCodeLogo` and `CodexLogo` SVG marks following the existing `AnthropicLogo`/`OpenAILogo` pattern (lines 37-70; each is `function XLogo({ size = 16, className }: AdapterLogoProps)`). Register them in `ADAPTER_LOGO_MAP` (line 134). Codex is OpenAI's — reuse/adapt `OpenAILogo` styling; OpenCode needs its own mark.
- `apps/client/src/layers/entities/runtime/config/runtime-descriptors.ts` (NEW) — export a `RuntimeDescriptor` type `{ type: string; label: string; icon: React.ComponentType<{ size?: number; className?: string }>; accent: string }` and a `RUNTIME_DESCRIPTORS: Record<string, RuntimeDescriptor>` for `claude-code | opencode | codex | test-mode`. Export a `getRuntimeDescriptor(type: string): RuntimeDescriptor` helper that returns a NEUTRAL fallback (generic icon, the raw type as label) for unknown types — never throw, never render blank. This supersedes the ad-hoc `RUNTIME_LABELS` map in `apps/client/.../agent-hub/ui/tabs/ConfigTab.tsx:25`; refactor ConfigTab to consume `getRuntimeDescriptor(type).label`.
- `apps/client/src/layers/entities/runtime/index.ts` — export `RUNTIME_DESCRIPTORS`, `getRuntimeDescriptor`, and the `RuntimeDescriptor` type (add to the existing barrel which currently exports only the capability hooks). This is the entities layer — it may import `@dorkos/icons` and `@/layers/shared`, nothing higher (FSD rule).
- Session-list rows: `apps/client/src/layers/features/session-list/ui/SessionSidebar.tsx` (and/or the row component it renders) — render a small runtime icon next to each session title from `getRuntimeDescriptor(session.runtime).icon`, with a tooltip showing `.label`. Keep it subtle (spec UX: "session list rows carry the runtime mark").

ACCEPTANCE:
- One import surface (`getRuntimeDescriptor`) yields the same icon/label/accent everywhere; unknown runtime type renders the neutral fallback, not a crash.
- ConfigTab's runtime select labels now come from the descriptor registry (no duplicate label map).
- Session rows show the runtime mark; with one runtime the mark is unobtrusive.

TESTS (`apps/client/.../entities/runtime/__tests__/` + session-list `__tests__/`): `getRuntimeDescriptor('opencode')` returns the OpenCode descriptor; `getRuntimeDescriptor('made-up')` returns the fallback; render a session row with `runtime: 'codex'` and assert the Codex label/tooltip. Use the mock Transport + Wrapper pattern from `.claude/rules/testing.md`.
tasks.activeForm: Adding the RuntimeDescriptor registry and icons

tasks.subject: [additional-agent-runtimes] [P1] Add the status-bar runtime chip (read-only after start, selectable pre-launch)
tasks.description:
Add a runtime chip to the chat status bar, mirroring the `PermissionModeItem` pattern, wired through the status-bar registry. Read-only once a session has started (ADR-0255 immutability); selectable in the pre-first-message state.

FILES:
- `apps/client/src/layers/features/status/ui/RuntimeItem.tsx` (NEW) — model it on `apps/client/src/layers/features/status/ui/PermissionModeItem.tsx` (a `StatusLine` item with a trigger button + dropdown). Props: `{ sessionId?: string; runtime: string; onChangeRuntime?: (type: string) => void; canSelect: boolean }`. Render the icon+label from `getRuntimeDescriptor(runtime)` (entities/runtime barrel — features may import entities). When `canSelect` is false (session started), render the chip read-only (no dropdown, like `PermissionModeItem`'s `disabled` branch with a tooltip "Runtime is fixed once a session starts"). When `canSelect` is true, render a dropdown of REGISTERED runtimes; source the selectable list from `useRuntimeCapabilities()` (`transport.getCapabilities()` returns `{ capabilities: Record<type, ...>, defaultRuntime }`) so only registered runtimes appear.
- `apps/client/src/layers/features/status/index.ts` — export `RuntimeItem`.
- Status-bar registry (`STATUS_BAR_REGISTRY`, exported from the status barrel; defined near the config popover) — add a `runtime` entry (key `'runtime'`, a label like "Runtime") so the item participates in the configure/hide machinery like `cwd`/`git`/`permission`.
- `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/ui/status/ChatStatusSection.tsx` — render `<StatusLine.Item itemKey="runtime" ...>` beside the model picker (the `ModelConfigPopover` block at lines 323-340), gated on a `showStatusBarRuntime` app-store flag (add the flag + setter to `useAppStore` alongside the sibling `showStatusBar*` flags). Determine `canSelect` from whether the session has started (reuse the same signal that disables `PermissionModeItem` via `disabled={!sessionId}` / hasStarted).

UX (spec): "the chat status bar shows the runtime chip"; only render the SELECTABLE dropdown when >1 runtime is registered — with one runtime, show a read-only chip ("with one runtime, DorkOS looks exactly as it does today").

ACCEPTANCE:
- Started session: chip is read-only and shows the correct runtime identity.
- Pre-first-message session with >1 registered runtime: chip is a dropdown of registered runtimes.
- With exactly one registered runtime: no dropdown affordance.

TESTS (`apps/client/.../status/__tests__/`): render `RuntimeItem` with `canSelect=false` -> no dropdown, tooltip present; with `canSelect=true` + a mock `getCapabilities` returning two runtimes -> dropdown lists both; changing selection calls `onChangeRuntime`. Mock Transport per `.claude/rules/testing.md`.

tasks.subject: [additional-agent-runtimes] [P1] Thread a ?runtime= launch param into new-session creation
tasks.description:
Let a new session be launched on a chosen runtime via a `?runtime=` URL search param, threaded into the first message POST as the existing `runtime` hint (which the server already honors and 400s on unknown types).

FILES:
- `apps/client/src/router.tsx` — `sessionSearchSchema` is at lines 46-54 (`z.object({ session: z.string().optional(), dir: z.string().optional(), ... })`). Add `runtime: z.string().optional()`. The `sessionRouteLoader` (lines ~104-142) already reads `session`/`dir` from `URLSearchParams`; it does not need to consume `runtime` (the loader only handles redirect/UUID minting) but the schema addition makes the param first-class and preserved across redirects (pass `runtime` through in the `redirect({ search: {...} })` calls so it survives the auto-select/UUID redirects).
- The first-message send path — the client already sends a `runtime` hint on `POST /api/sessions/:id/messages` (server side `resolveRuntimeTypeForNewSession` at `apps/server/src/routes/sessions.ts:291-326` reads `body.runtime` first). Locate where the client builds the first send body (grep the chat send hook / transport `sendMessage` call) and pass the `?runtime=` search param as the `runtime` field on the FIRST turn only (subsequent turns must not resend it — `persistSessionRuntime` is first-write-wins, so it is harmless but noise). Read the param via TanStack Router's search on the `/session` route.
- `apps/client/src/layers/features/agents-list/ui/SessionLaunchPopover.tsx` — when launching a session FROM an agent, pass the agent's runtime as `?runtime=` (spec UX: "SessionLaunchPopover passes the agent's runtime"; "Launching from an agent pre-selects the agent's runtime"). The server-side manifest fallback already resolves this, so this is about MAKING IT VISIBLE via the param + chip (task 1.7), not new server behavior.

SERVER: no change needed — `body.runtime` hint is already accepted and unknown-runtime already 400s (spec §API changes). Do NOT modify the POST handler.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Visiting `/session?runtime=codex` and sending the first message creates a session bound to `codex` (when registered) — verifiable via `session_metadata.runtime` and the read-only chip after start.
- The param survives the loader's auto-select/UUID redirects.
- Launching from an agent carries that agent's runtime into the new session.

TESTS: `router.tsx` search-schema test — `sessionSearchSchema.parse({ runtime: 'opencode' })` succeeds and `parse({})` leaves it undefined; extend `SessionLaunchPopover.test.tsx` to assert an agent launch navigates with `search.runtime` set to the agent's runtime.

tasks.subject: [additional-agent-runtimes] [P2] Add the Codex SDK dependency, ESLint confinement, and checkDependencies
tasks.description:
Bring in the Codex SDK behind an ESLint boundary and implement the dependency/auth check — the foundation the rest of the Codex adapter builds on.

FILES:
- `apps/server/package.json` — add `"@openai/codex-sdk": "~0.142.5"` PINNED (Apache-2.0; near-continuous release cadence with occasional breaking changes per ADR-0307, so pin, do not caret-range loosely). Run `pnpm install`.
- `packages/eslint-config/` — add a `no-restricted-imports` entry mirroring the existing Claude-SDK rule: `@openai/codex-sdk` may be imported ONLY under `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/codex/`. (Hard Rule #2: SDK imports confined.)
- `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/codex/codex-runtime.ts` (NEW, stub for now) — a `CodexRuntime implements AgentRuntime` skeleton with `readonly type = 'codex'` and `checkDependencies()` implemented; other methods can throw `not implemented` until later tasks fill them (or delegate to shared EventLog helpers as they land).
- `checkDependencies()` (in the codex adapter) — verify (a) the `codex` CLI binary is resolvable (config `runtimes.codex.binaryPath` or PATH) and (b) login state exists (`codex login` — ChatGPT OAuth or `CODEX_API_KEY`). Return `DependencyCheck[]` with `installHint` copy: `npm i -g @openai/codex && codex login` and `infoUrl` `https://developers.openai.com/codex`. Shape per `packages/shared/src/agent-runtime.ts` `DependencyCheck` (name/description/status/version?/requiredVersion?/installHint?/infoUrl?).
- Read binary path + enabled flag from `runtimes.codex` config (task 1.2). Build any child-process args from validated config only — no shell interpolation (spec §Security).

ACCEPTANCE:
- `pnpm lint` fails if `@openai/codex-sdk` is imported outside the codex adapter dir.
- `checkDependencies()` returns `status: 'missing'` with the install hint when the binary/login is absent, `'satisfied'` when present.
- `GET /api/system/requirements` (which already aggregates all registered runtimes) surfaces the Codex checks once the runtime is registered.

TESTS (`apps/server/src/services/runtimes/codex/__tests__/`): mock the binary-resolution + login probe; assert `checkDependencies` shapes for satisfied/missing. Never require the real `codex` binary.
tasks.activeForm: Adding Codex SDK dependency and confinement

tasks.subject: [additional-agent-runtimes] [P2] Verify Codex permission/sandbox/approval mapping against the real SDK (carried-to-EXECUTE)
tasks.description:
Carried-to-EXECUTE verification item (spec Open Questions: "Permission-mode persistence ... decided against real SDK behavior during Phase 2/3, additive-only either way"). This is a VERIFICATION, not a design decision. Do it EARLY in Phase 2, before the event mapper and facade lock in a mapping.

WORK:
- Against a locally-installed `codex` + `@openai/codex-sdk@~0.142.5`, run `startThread`/`runStreamed` with EXPLICIT sandbox/approval params (post-0.132.0 requires them — no implicit defaults, ADR-0307) and record: the exact approval-level values the SDK accepts, the sandbox param shape, and how tool-approval requests surface in the `runStreamed` event stream (which of the 7 event types carries them).
- Map Codex's approval levels onto DorkOS `RuntimeCapabilities.permissionModes` (`PermissionModeDescriptor[]` with `id`+`label`). Default CONSERVATIVELY (approval-required modes, matching Codex's own default posture — spec §Security).
- Decide whether the mapping fits existing `PermissionModeSchema` values (`packages/shared/src/schemas.ts`) or needs ADDITIVE enum members. If additive members are required (only when a mode must PERSIST in `session_metadata` settings), add them additively — never remove or repurpose existing values. Most runtimes declare modes via `RuntimeCapabilities.permissionModes` WITHOUT touching the shared enum (the test-mode pattern); prefer that.
- Record the interrupt/abort surface the SDK exposes (for `interruptQuery` in task 2.5).

DELIVERABLE: a short findings note appended to the adapter's directory as a code comment or a `NOTES.md` in `services/runtimes/codex/`, plus the concrete `permissionModes` descriptor array the `CodexRuntime.getCapabilities()` will return (consumed by tasks 2.4/2.5). If the June-2026 `logs_2.sqlite` unbounded-write CLI defect (ADR-0307) is still unpatched at the pinned version, note the config mitigation.

ACCEPTANCE:
- The permission-mode descriptor array for Codex is finalized and justified against observed SDK behavior.
- Any enum change is additive-only; existing `PermissionModeSchema` values are untouched.
- Interrupt mechanism is documented for the facade task.

NOTE: this runs against real binaries LOCALLY (not CI). If binaries are unavailable in the execution environment, record the SDK's TypeScript types + published docs as the evidence basis and flag for a follow-up live re-verification.
tasks.activeForm: Verifying Codex permission mapping against the SDK

tasks.subject: [additional-agent-runtimes] [P2] Persist the Codex sessionId <-> threadId mapping
tasks.description:
Implement the adapter-owned durable map from a DorkOS `sessionId` to a Codex `threadId` (ADR-0307: one DorkOS session <-> one Codex thread). This MUST keep `session_metadata` untouched.

DECISION (assumption logged): implement as a NEW SQLite table `codex_threads (sessionId PK, threadId, createdAt)` in `packages/db` (the spec offers this OR a `features`-scoped metadata blob "decided at implementation by whichever keeps `session_metadata` untouched"). A dedicated table is the explicit, testable choice and leaves `session_metadata` (ADR-0255/0260 immutable runtime binding + mutable settings) completely untouched.

FILES:
- `packages/db/src/schema/` — add a `codexThreads` Drizzle table (`sessionId: text primary key`, `threadId: text notNull`, `createdAt: text notNull`), export it from the package barrel, and add the migration the repo's Drizzle setup uses (follow how `sessionMetadata` in `packages/db/src/schema/sessions.ts` is defined + migrated).
- `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/codex/thread-map.ts` (NEW) — a small class/module: `getThreadId(sessionId): string | undefined`, `setThreadId(sessionId, threadId): void` (INSERT OR IGNORE / first-write-wins, mirroring `persistSessionRuntime`'s `onConflictDoNothing`), taking the `Db` handle injected at construction. No `os.homedir()`; receive `dorkHome`/`Db` per `.claude/rules/dork-home.md`.

ACCEPTANCE:
- `setThreadId` then `getThreadId` round-trips; a second `setThreadId` for the same session is a no-op (first-write-wins).
- `session_metadata` schema is unchanged; no dual-write to it.

TESTS (`apps/server/src/services/runtimes/codex/__tests__/thread-map.test.ts`): against an in-memory/temp Drizzle DB, assert round-trip + idempotency + `undefined` for an unknown session. Follow service-test patterns in `.claude/rules/testing.md`.

tasks.subject: [additional-agent-runtimes] [P2] Map Codex runStreamed events to StreamEvent with fixtures
tasks.description:
Build the event mapper translating the Codex SDK's `runStreamed()` events (7 structured types) into DorkOS's shared `StreamEvent` vocabulary, tested purely against recorded fixtures (no binary in CI).

FILES:
- `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/codex/event-mapper.ts` (NEW) — pure functions mapping each Codex event type to the corresponding `StreamEvent` (`packages/shared/src/types` / the StreamEvent union used by the Claude mapper). Cover: text/assistant deltas, tool calls, tool-approval requests (surfaced via the `supportsToolApproval` flow -> `question_prompt`/approval events consistent with how the Claude adapter maps them), errors (typed error event), and interrupt/turn-end (a TERMINAL event so the conformance suite's "ends in a terminal event" assertion holds). Use the approval/permission mapping finalized in task 2.2.
- `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/codex/__tests__/codex-scenarios.ts` (NEW) — recorded/scripted Codex SDK event fixtures + a `vi.mock('@openai/codex-sdk')` helper with scripted thread events, following the conventions in `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/claude-code/__tests__/sdk-scenarios.ts` (which lives inside the SDK ESLint boundary). Include error, interrupt, and tool-approval shapes (spec §Testing Strategy: "event mappers (fixture SDK/SSE events -> StreamEvent, including error/interrupt/tool-approval shapes)").
- `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/codex/__tests__/event-mapper.test.ts` (NEW).

ACCEPTANCE:
- Every Codex event type maps to a well-formed `StreamEvent`; a full scripted turn ends in a terminal event.
- Error and interrupt events map to typed error / turn-end events, not silent drops.
- No `@openai/codex-sdk` import escapes the codex adapter dir (ESLint from 2.1).

TESTS: fixture-driven mapper unit tests as above; assert exact `StreamEvent` shapes for text, tool-call, tool-approval, error, and terminal cases.

tasks.subject: [additional-agent-runtimes] [P2] Complete the CodexRuntime facade and register it in the composition root
tasks.description:
Assemble the full `CodexRuntime implements AgentRuntime` from the thread map, event mapper, and capability mapping, and register it so Codex sessions work end-to-end (create -> send -> stream -> interrupt -> history -> listed).

FILES:
- `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/codex/codex-runtime.ts` — flesh out the facade:
  - `ensureSession(sessionId, opts)` -> resolve the thread map (task 2.3): existing threadId -> `resumeThread(threadId)`, else `startThread()` and `setThreadId`. Pass EXPLICIT sandbox/approval params (post-0.132.0 contract, ADR-0307) derived from the resolved permission mode.
  - `sendMessage(sessionId, content, opts)` -> drive `runStreamed()`, feed its events through the event mapper (task 2.4) into `StreamEvent`s. Follow the test-mode pattern (EventLog + `SessionStateProjector`) for live turn state so `/events` delivery works via the existing projector path (`getOrCreateProjector`, `subscribeSession`, `getSessionSnapshot` like `TestModeRuntime` at lines 249-285).
  - `interruptQuery(sessionId)` -> the SDK abort surface identified in task 2.2.
  - `getMessageHistory`/`getSession`/`listSessions` -> read thread state under `~/.codex/sessions` VIA THE SDK, not by scanning files (ADR-0307). Set `runtime: 'codex'` on returned `Session` objects (task 1.1 required field).
  - `getCapabilities()` -> return the finalized `RuntimeCapabilities` for Codex (permissionModes from task 2.2; `supportsCostTracking`, `supportsMcp`, `supportsPlugins`, `supportsQuestionPrompt`, `supportsResume` set honestly per real SDK support — plugins false, resume true).
  - `getSupportedModels()` -> the models the Codex CLI exposes.
  - `approveTool`/`submitAnswers` -> wire tool-approval to the SDK's approval surface.
- `apps/server/src/index.ts` — register `CodexRuntime` in the composition root (near where runtimes are registered, ~lines 194-218 per ideation). Register only when `runtimes.codex.enabled` (task 1.2 config). Inject `Db` into the thread map.

DEPENDS ON: thread map (2.3), event mapper (2.4), verification (2.2), config (1.2), Session.runtime (1.1), conformance suite (1.5, which this must pass).

ACCEPTANCE:
- A Codex session: create -> send -> streamed response in chat -> interrupt -> history on revisit -> appears in the aggregated session list tagged `runtime: 'codex'`.
- `runtimeConformance(() => new CodexRuntime(...deps))` passes with the SDK mocked (task 2.6 wires the test file).
- Capability-gated UI adapts (no cost strip if `supportsCostTracking` false, permission picker shows Codex's modes).

TESTS: covered by 2.6 (conformance + integration). This task must leave those green.

tasks.subject: [additional-agent-runtimes] [P2] Wire Codex conformance + integration tests and a local e2e smoke
tasks.description:
Prove the Codex adapter against the shared conformance suite and the aggregation/SSE integration paths, all with mocked backends; add a local-only real-CLI smoke.

FILES:
- `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/codex/__tests__/conformance.test.ts` (NEW) — `runtimeConformance(() => new CodexRuntime(...deps))` (suite from task 1.5) with `@openai/codex-sdk` mocked via the scenarios helper (task 2.4). Must be green.
- Integration (`apps/server/src/routes/__tests__/`) — extend task 1.3's aggregated-listing test to register Codex + claude-code (or test-mode) and assert Codex sessions merge + are tagged; assert SSE turn delivery through the projector for a mocked Codex turn (use `collectSseEvents` from `@dorkos/test-utils` per `.claude/rules/testing.md`); assert the `runtime` hint 400s on an unknown type (extend the existing test).
- E2E smoke (local, env-gated): document/enable running the conformance/adapter against the REAL `codex` CLI locally via an env flag (e.g. the same `optionally against live binaries locally via env flag` hook the conformance suite exposes in task 1.5). CI never requires the binary.

ACCEPTANCE:
- `pnpm vitest run` green for the Codex conformance + integration tests with all backends mocked.
- Aggregated `GET /api/sessions` returns Codex sessions tagged `runtime: 'codex'` with one-runtime-failing degradation intact.
- Local live-binary run (when available) exercises a real Codex turn end-to-end.

TESTS: this task IS the test wiring; verify green and that the SSE integration asserts at least one `text_delta`-equivalent event and a terminal event for a Codex turn.
tasks.activeForm: Wiring Codex conformance and integration tests

tasks.subject: [additional-agent-runtimes] [P3] Add the OpenCode SDK dependency, ESLint confinement, config, and checkDependencies
tasks.description:
Bring in the OpenCode SDK behind an ESLint boundary, wire its config block, and implement the dependency/auth check — the foundation for the sidecar and mappers.

FILES:
- `apps/server/package.json` — add `"@opencode-ai/sdk": "^1.17.13"` (MIT; REST + SSE client for `opencode serve`, OpenAPI 3.1 at `/doc`). Run `pnpm install`.
- `packages/eslint-config/` — add a `no-restricted-imports` entry: `@opencode-ai/sdk` importable ONLY under `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/opencode/` (Hard Rule #2, mirroring the Claude + Codex rules).
- `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/opencode/opencode-runtime.ts` (NEW stub) — `OpenCodeRuntime implements AgentRuntime` skeleton, `readonly type = 'opencode'`, `checkDependencies()` implemented; other methods stubbed until later tasks.
- `checkDependencies()` — verify (a) the `opencode` binary is resolvable (config `runtimes.opencode.binaryPath` or PATH) and (b) that `opencode serve` can be reached / auth is present. Return `DependencyCheck[]` with `installHint` `npm i -g opencode-ai && opencode auth login` and `infoUrl` `https://opencode.ai/docs/server`.
- Read `runtimes.opencode` config (task 1.2): `enabled`, `binaryPath` (null -> PATH), `port` (0 -> ephemeral). Build child-process args from validated config only — no shell interpolation.

NOTE: `'opencode'` is already added to `AgentRuntimeSchema` in task 1.1 — do not re-add.

ACCEPTANCE:
- `pnpm lint` fails if `@opencode-ai/sdk` is imported outside the opencode adapter dir.
- `checkDependencies()` returns `'missing'` + install hint when the binary/auth is absent, `'satisfied'` when present; surfaced by `GET /api/system/requirements` once registered.

TESTS (`apps/server/src/services/runtimes/opencode/__tests__/`): mock binary resolution + a health probe; assert `checkDependencies` shapes. Never require the real `opencode` binary.
tasks.activeForm: Adding OpenCode SDK dependency and confinement

tasks.subject: [additional-agent-runtimes] [P3] Verify OpenCode sidecar x per-session cwd behavior against the real SDK (carried-to-EXECUTE)
tasks.description:
Carried-to-EXECUTE verification item (spec Open Questions): confirm the OpenCode SDK creates sessions with a PER-SESSION `cwd` on ONE `opencode serve` instance. This is a VERIFICATION with a defined fallback, not a redesign — it must run EARLY in Phase 3 because it decides the internal shape of the server-manager (3.3) and session-mapper (3.5).

WORK:
- Against a locally-installed `opencode` + `@opencode-ai/sdk@^1.17.13`, start one `opencode serve` and create two sessions with DIFFERENT working directories via the SDK. Confirm whether one server instance honors per-session `cwd`.
- IF per-session cwd works on a single instance: the adapter keeps ONE managed sidecar (ADR-0306 default). Document it.
- IF a server instance is directory-BOUND (cwd fixed per instance): fall back to a small PER-CWD instance POOL inside `server-manager.ts` (spec: "if a server instance is directory-bound, fall back to a small per-cwd instance pool ... Does not change the adapter's public shape"). Document the pool sizing/eviction sketch for task 3.3.
- Also record how OpenCode permission requests surface over SSE and the approval API (for the permission-forwarding in task 3.6 / mapper in task 3.4), and how OpenCode's permission modes map onto `RuntimeCapabilities.permissionModes` (expected: `default`/`acceptEdits`-like; additive-only enum changes if any must persist — same rule as task 2.2). Default conservatively (approval-required).

DELIVERABLE: a findings note (code comment or `NOTES.md` in `services/runtimes/opencode/`) stating the cwd verdict (single-instance vs per-cwd pool) and the OpenCode `permissionModes` descriptor array that `OpenCodeRuntime.getCapabilities()` will return.

ACCEPTANCE:
- The cwd question is answered with evidence; the server-manager design (single vs pool) is chosen and justified.
- The OpenCode permission-mode descriptor array is finalized; any shared-enum change is additive-only.

NOTE: runs against real binaries LOCALLY. If unavailable in the execution environment, base the verdict on the SDK types + `opencode.ai/docs/server` and flag for live re-verification, defaulting to the single-instance design with the per-cwd pool as the documented contingency.

tasks.subject: [additional-agent-runtimes] [P3] Build the opencode serve sidecar server-manager (spawn/health/backoff/shutdown)
tasks.description:
Implement the managed `opencode serve` sidecar lifecycle (ADR-0306): lazily spawned, health-checked, restarted with exponential backoff, torn down on server shutdown, bound to localhost with per-boot auth.

FILES:
- `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/opencode/server-manager.ts` (NEW):
  - Lazy spawn on first OpenCode use; `binaryPath`/`port` from `runtimes.opencode` config (task 1.2; `port: 0` -> ephemeral, read back the chosen port).
  - Bind `127.0.0.1` ONLY, with `OPENCODE_SERVER_PASSWORD` basic auth from a generated per-boot secret; never exposed through the DorkOS tunnel (spec §Security).
  - Health check before use; exponential-backoff restart on crash; kill/orphan-cleanup on DorkOS server shutdown.
  - If task 3.2 found instances are directory-bound, implement the small PER-CWD instance pool here (keyed by cwd, bounded, evictable); otherwise a single managed instance. Keep the public surface (a method that returns a ready SDK client for a given cwd) identical either way.
  - Cold sidecar startup (~1-3s) is lazy and MUST NOT block session listing — listing reports empty + warning until healthy (coordinated with task 1.3's per-runtime timeout).
- Wire shutdown into the server's teardown path (the same place the tunnel/other lifecycles are torn down in `index.ts`).

ACCEPTANCE:
- Sidecar spawns lazily, becomes healthy, and is reachable only on `127.0.0.1` with the generated password.
- A crashed sidecar restarts with backoff; DorkOS shutdown kills it (no orphan).
- Startup never blocks the session list.

TESTS (`apps/server/src/services/runtimes/opencode/__tests__/server-manager.test.ts`): drive spawn/health/backoff/shutdown with a FAKE child process (spec §Testing: "server-manager lifecycle (spawn/health/backoff/shutdown with a fake child process)"). Assert backoff schedule, restart-on-exit, and clean shutdown. Never spawn a real `opencode`.
tasks.activeForm: Building the opencode serve sidecar manager

tasks.subject: [additional-agent-runtimes] [P3] Map OpenCode SSE events to StreamEvent with recorded fixtures
tasks.description:
Build the event mapper translating OpenCode's server SSE event stream into DorkOS `StreamEvent`s, tested against recorded fixtures via a fake SSE server (no binary in CI).

FILES:
- `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/opencode/event-mapper.ts` (NEW) — pure functions mapping OpenCode SSE events to `StreamEvent`s. The server multiplexes ALL sessions on one event stream, so the mapper/filter must key events to the right DorkOS session (spec §Performance: "OpenCode multiplexes sessions on one event stream" -> one subscription per runtime, filter per session). Cover: assistant/text deltas, tool calls, permission requests (surfaced through the `supportsToolApproval` -> `approveTool` flow), errors (typed error event), and interrupt/turn-end terminal events. Use the permission mapping from task 3.2.
- `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/opencode/__tests__/opencode-sse-fixtures.ts` (NEW) — recorded fixture SSE events + a fake SSE server helper emitting them (spec §Mocking: "OpenCode: fake SSE server emitting recorded fixtures"). Include error, interrupt, and tool-approval shapes.
- `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/opencode/__tests__/event-mapper.test.ts` (NEW).

ACCEPTANCE:
- Each OpenCode SSE event type maps to a well-formed `StreamEvent`; a scripted turn ends in a terminal event; events are correctly attributed to their session when the stream is multiplexed.
- Permission requests map to the tool-approval flow; errors map to typed error events.
- No `@opencode-ai/sdk` import escapes the adapter dir (ESLint from 3.1).

TESTS: fixture-driven mapper unit tests via the fake SSE server; assert per-session filtering with two interleaved sessions on one stream, plus exact shapes for text/tool/approval/error/terminal cases.
tasks.activeForm: Mapping OpenCode SSE events to StreamEvent

tasks.subject: [additional-agent-runtimes] [P3] Build the OpenCode session-mapper (session <-> OpenCode session, list/history via SDK)
tasks.description:
Map DorkOS sessions 1:1 to OpenCode sessions and read listing/history through the SDK — never by scanning OpenCode's SQLite store (ADR-0306: its store is opaque, runtime-owned).

FILES:
- `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/opencode/session-mapper.ts` (NEW):
  - Create/resolve an OpenCode session for a DorkOS `sessionId`, threading the per-session `cwd` (single instance or per-cwd pool from task 3.2/3.3).
  - `listSessions(projectDir)` and `getMessageHistory(projectDir, sessionId)` read via `@opencode-ai/sdk` REST calls against the managed server (task 3.3). Set `runtime: 'opencode'` on returned `Session` objects (task 1.1 required field).
  - Keep the mapping between DorkOS session ids and OpenCode session ids (adapter-owned; in-memory is acceptable since the OpenCode server is the source of truth and can be re-listed — document the choice).

ACCEPTANCE:
- A DorkOS session maps to exactly one OpenCode session; history/list come exclusively through the SDK (no direct DB read).
- Returned sessions are tagged `runtime: 'opencode'` and merge correctly into the aggregated list (task 1.3).

TESTS (`apps/server/src/services/runtimes/opencode/__tests__/session-mapper.test.ts`): with a mocked SDK client, assert `listSessions`/`getMessageHistory` call the SDK (not the filesystem) and tag `runtime`; assert 1:1 mapping stability across calls. Never touch a real OpenCode DB.

tasks.subject: [additional-agent-runtimes] [P3] Complete the OpenCodeRuntime facade and register it in the composition root
tasks.description:
Assemble the full `OpenCodeRuntime implements AgentRuntime` from the sidecar manager, SSE mapper, and session mapper, and register it so OpenCode sessions work end-to-end.

FILES:
- `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/opencode/opencode-runtime.ts` — flesh out the facade:
  - `ensureSession`/`sendMessage` -> use the session-mapper (3.5) to resolve the OpenCode session, drive a turn on the sidecar (3.3), subscribe to the server's SSE stream (ONE subscription per runtime), filter per session, and feed mapped events (3.4) into the shared EventLog + `SessionStateProjector` so `/events` delivery rides the existing projector path (mirror `TestModeRuntime` snapshot/subscribe at lines 249-285).
  - Permissions: OpenCode permission requests surface via `supportsToolApproval` -> `approveTool` forwards the decision to the SDK.
  - `interruptQuery` -> the SDK/HTTP interrupt surface.
  - `getMessageHistory`/`listSessions`/`getSession` -> delegate to the session-mapper (3.5).
  - `getCapabilities()` -> honest `RuntimeCapabilities` for OpenCode (permissionModes from task 3.2; `supportsMcp`, `supportsResume`, `supportsCostTracking`, `supportsPlugins`, `supportsQuestionPrompt` per real support; plugins false).
  - `getSupportedModels()` -> the providers/models OpenCode exposes (including local models via Ollama / OpenAI-compatible endpoints — the open-source-model constraint).
- `apps/server/src/index.ts` — register `OpenCodeRuntime` in the composition root when `runtimes.opencode.enabled` (task 1.2). Ensure the sidecar shutdown is wired into server teardown (task 3.3).

DEPENDS ON: sidecar (3.3), SSE mapper (3.4), session-mapper (3.5), verification (3.2), config (1.2), Session.runtime (1.1), conformance (1.5).

ACCEPTANCE:
- An OpenCode session: create -> send -> streamed response in chat -> interrupt -> history on revisit -> appears in the aggregated list tagged `runtime: 'opencode'`.
- Permission requests reach the approval UI; a mid-session sidecar crash surfaces a typed error event and the session shows a retry affordance while the sidecar restarts with backoff.
- Capability-gated UI adapts to OpenCode's declared capabilities.

TESTS: covered by 3.7 (conformance + integration + local-model). This task must leave those green.

tasks.subject: [additional-agent-runtimes] [P3] Wire OpenCode conformance + integration tests and verify the local-model path
tasks.description:
Prove the OpenCode adapter against the shared conformance suite and the aggregation/SSE integration paths (mocked), and verify the open-source/local-model path against a real Ollama model locally.

FILES:
- `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/opencode/__tests__/conformance.test.ts` (NEW) — `runtimeConformance(() => new OpenCodeRuntime(...deps))` (suite from task 1.5) with the sidecar/SSE mocked via the fake SSE server + a mocked SDK client (fixtures from task 3.4). Must be green.
- Integration (`apps/server/src/routes/__tests__/`) — extend task 1.3's aggregated-listing test to register OpenCode; assert OpenCode sessions merge + are tagged, one-runtime-failing (cold sidecar) yields partial list + warning (spec §Performance: "cold OpenCode sidecar must not block listing"); assert SSE turn delivery through the projector for a mocked OpenCode turn (`collectSseEvents`).
- Local-model verification (local, env-gated): with a real `opencode` pointed at a local Ollama model (e.g. a `qwen2.5-coder-32b`-class model per spec §Implementation Phases), run a real turn end-to-end and confirm streamed output. CI never requires the binary or Ollama.

ACCEPTANCE:
- `pnpm vitest run` green for OpenCode conformance + integration with all backends mocked.
- Aggregated `GET /api/sessions` returns OpenCode sessions tagged `runtime: 'opencode'` with cold-sidecar degradation intact.
- Local run against an open-source model via OpenCode+Ollama streams a real response (satisfies the brief's open-source-model constraint).

TESTS: this task IS the test wiring; verify green and that the SSE integration asserts a streamed delta + terminal event for an OpenCode turn.
tasks.activeForm: Wiring OpenCode conformance and local-model tests

tasks.subject: [additional-agent-runtimes] [P4] Add the 'needs setup' runtime state and 'Add a runtime' requirements flow
tasks.description:
Make a registered-but-unsatisfied runtime (binary missing, not logged in) a first-class GUIDED state in the picker — never a dead dropdown or a raw stack trace (spec §UX "Runtime not ready").

FILES:
- Reuse the `SystemRequirementsStep` machinery (onboarding) as a settings-surface component. Locate `SystemRequirementsStep` (renders per-runtime `checkDependencies` results with install hints) and extract/reuse it so the runtime picker can open a requirements panel for a specific runtime.
- Runtime picker (`RuntimeItem.tsx` from task 1.7 and the `SessionLaunchPopover` path from task 1.8): when a KNOWN runtime is registered but its `checkDependencies` is unsatisfied, show it in a "needs setup" state; selecting it opens the requirements panel with COPYABLE commands: `npm i -g opencode-ai && opencode auth login` (OpenCode) and `npm i -g @openai/codex && codex login` (Codex). Also provide an "Add a runtime" entry point in the picker when a known runtime is unregistered/missing deps.
- Mid-session failure: when a runtime breaks mid-session (sidecar crash, CLI exit), the turn fails with a TYPED error event (adapters already emit this per tasks 2.4/3.4) and the session shows a RETRY affordance. Ensure the chat surface renders the retry affordance for the typed error event.
- A runtime appears as a SELECTABLE (non-setup) option only when registered AND `checkDependencies` passes; otherwise the setup affordance (spec §Implementation approach).

ACCEPTANCE:
- An unsatisfied runtime shows a needs-setup affordance with copyable install/auth commands, not a dead option or stack trace.
- Mid-session runtime failure shows a retry affordance tied to the typed error event.

TESTS (`apps/client/.../status/__tests__/` or picker tests): a runtime whose `checkDependencies` returns `'missing'` renders the needs-setup state + copyable command; a satisfied runtime renders selectable. Mock Transport `getCapabilities`/requirements per `.claude/rules/testing.md`.

tasks.subject: [additional-agent-runtimes] [P4] Runtime-named empty/error/loading copy pass across capability-gated surfaces
tasks.description:
Do a copy pass so every empty/error/loading state NAMES the runtime and every capability gate is honest (spec §UX "Capability honesty").

WORK:
- Error/empty states name the runtime: e.g. "OpenCode server is starting...", "Codex requires login" (spec examples). Audit the chat surface, session list, and status bar for generic "loading"/"error" copy that should be runtime-specific when a non-default runtime is active.
- Verify capability gates render honestly against all three real profiles: cost strip only when `supportsCostTracking`; permission-mode picker shows the runtime's declared modes (already wired via `useActiveCapabilities` -> `PermissionModeItem`); plugin/command surfaces gate on their flags; `supportsQuestionPrompt`/`supportsMcp` gates hold. This is a VERIFICATION + copy task, not new gating (the gates already exist per `useActiveCapabilities`).
- Use `getRuntimeDescriptor(type).label` (task 1.6) for the runtime name in copy so it stays consistent.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Non-default-runtime error/empty/loading states name the runtime.
- Cost/permission/plugin/MCP/question gates verified against Claude-code, Codex, and OpenCode capability profiles (three real profiles per spec §Goals).

TESTS: extend capability-gated component tests to assert a gated surface (e.g. cost strip) is absent for a `supportsCostTracking: false` runtime and present for one that supports it; assert a runtime-named error string renders for a starting/unauthed runtime. Mock Transport capabilities per runtime.

tasks.subject: [additional-agent-runtimes] [P4] Add Playwright e2e for runtime picker, launch param, chip, and list badges

tasks.subject: [additional-agent-runtimes] [P4] Write contributing/adding-a-runtime.md (the runtime-author guide)
tasks.description:
Write the DX guide that makes runtime #4 a checklist (spec §Documentation). Follow the `writing-developer-guides` skill.

FILE:

  - `AgentRuntime` interface walk-through (`packages/shared/src/agent-runtime.ts`): session lifecycle, `sendMessage` -> `StreamEvent`, interactive flows, storage queries, locking, `RuntimeCapabilities` (structured `permissionModes`, `features` map).

- The guide references the real files/exports shipped by this spec.

tasks.subject: [additional-agent-runtimes] [P4] Write the Runtimes user guide and update AGENTS.md, architecture, and config docs
tasks.description:
Write the user-facing runtimes docs and update the internal references (spec §Documentation).

FILES:
- `docs/` (Fumadocs, rendered by `apps/site`) — a "Runtimes" user guide: installing/connecting OpenCode and Codex, choosing a runtime (per-agent + per-session + config default `runtimes.default`), and local models via OpenCode + Ollama. Copyable install/auth commands (`npm i -g opencode-ai && opencode auth login`, `npm i -g @openai/codex && codex login`).
- `AGENTS.md` — update the runtimes list (the doc currently frames Claude Code as the runtime; reflect OpenCode + Codex as production runtimes and the conformance/adding-a-runtime rails).
- `contributing/architecture.md` — add/refresh the adapter diagram showing claude-code / test-mode / opencode / codex under `services/runtimes/`, the sidecar for OpenCode, and the SDK-thread model for Codex.
